mechanism_narrative: A persistent increase in real retail sales (RSAFS) signals robust consumer spending, which drives corporate revenues and earnings higher, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days. The relation is monotonic and does not require a threshold to activate.
failure_condition: If RSAFS increases month-over-month and the S&P 500 forward 20-day return is negative in more than 55% of such instances, or if the correlation between RSAFS change and forward returns is not statistically significant (p > 0.05) over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.5%, the cost of capital becomes restrictive enough to dampen investment and consumer spending, reducing corporate earnings and causing negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If after FEDFUNDS crosses above 4.5%, the median forward 20-day return is positive or if the hit rate of negative returns is below 60% over the full out-of-sample period.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in industrial production (INDPRO) coinciding with an increase in retail sales (RSAFS) indicates an inventory buildup that will force future production cuts, leading to lower corporate profits and negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 days. The joint signal is necessary because each series alone may be ambiguous.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month decline and RSAFS month-over-month increase
failure_condition: If the joint condition occurs but forward returns are positive more than 50% of the time, or if the interaction term in a linear regression (INDPRO_change * RSAFS_change) is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in predicting returns.

mechanism_narrative: During the late-cycle expansion phase when the unemployment rate is already low (below 5%), any rise in the unemployment rate signals an imminent recession as the labor market softens from full employment, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days. In early recovery, rising unemployment from labor force growth is not bearish.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises in a month when it is below 5%
failure_condition: If in months where UNRATE is below 5% and increases, the forward 60-day return is positive in more than 60% of cases, or the conditional average return is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rising industrial production index (INDPRO) reflects expanding manufacturing output, which boosts corporate profitability and predicts positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-trading-day horizon. This signal is always active without any threshold.
failure_condition: If the one-month change in INDPRO is positively correlated with forward 60-day returns at less than 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window, or if the mean forward return after positive INDPRO changes is not significantly greater than zero.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp increase in the unemployment rate of more than 0.5 percentage points over a three-month period signals abrupt labor market deterioration, which reduces consumer income and spending, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.5pp in 3 months
failure_condition: If after such UNRATE increases, the average forward 20-day return is positive, or if the proportion of negative returns is less than 55%.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is very low (below 2%) and housing starts (HOUST) are elevated (above 1.5 million units annualized), it signals a housing market bubble that increases financial fragility. This fragile state predicts negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as risks of a correction are elevated.
regime_hint: accommodative monetary policy with housing boom
failure_condition: If the joint condition is met but the average forward 5-day return is positive, or if the signal does not produce a Sharpe ratio below zero over the out-of-sample period.

mechanism_narrative: During a disinflationary period when consumer price inflation is below 2% and falling, a reduction in the federal funds rate is highly stimulative for equities because it signals aggressive easing to combat deflation risks, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 days. In contrast, rate cuts during high inflation are contractionary for real returns.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS decreasing and CPIAUCSL year-over-year growth below 2%
failure_condition: If the joint condition of FEDFUNDS decline and low CPI inflation produces positive forward 20-day returns in less than 60% of cases, or if the conditional return is not significantly greater than the unconditional mean.
